title.line2: faster, safer, and with less work

hero.description: JsonTranslate supports JSON, Markdown, and TXT inputs. For JSON, it detects added, modified, and deleted content at the key level so only real changes are translated. Markdown and TXT go through the same workflow, making document translation easier to manage in one place.

stats.label: Input methods
stats.description: Bring JSON, Markdown, TXT, and other plain-text content directly into the workspace.

stats.label: Translation modes
stats.description: Cover single-file edits, project-scale delivery, and full re-translation workflows.

stats.label: Sync flow
stats.description: Keep one continuous translation loop from the hosted workspace to your local repository.

preview.title: Preview the latest responsive Web Studio
preview.modeBadge: Hosted by us / Bring your own key
preview.studioTitle: A responsive Web Studio designed for translation tasks

source.title: en / common.json

target.title: Multi-language tabs and side-by-side output

summary: Top-level actions keep upload, settings, task center, translation, and export in one place.
Project browser, source editor, and target output stay visible together for side-by-side work.
The layout stacks vertically on mobile and expands into a real three-column workspace on desktop.

capabilities.title: Six capabilities built around real translation workflows
capabilities.description: From incremental translation and path mapping to task management and CLI sync, every block maps to an actual localization workflow instead of generic marketing language.

cards.title: Multi-format input and smart incremental translation
cards.description: Built for real i18n workflows, not just structured language files. It also handles docs and plain-text content.
bullets: Upload, drag, or paste JSON, Markdown, and TXT files.
Support file-level incremental translation, full-file translation, project incremental translation, and full-project translation.
Use Git-diff-like change detection so only added or edited content gets translated.

cards.title: Project path mapping and path rules
cards.description: This is not a single text box translator. Source files, target files, and directory conventions are managed together at the project level.
bullets: Map project directories and generate corresponding locale files automatically after translation.
Support common patterns such as locale folders, module folders, and locale suffixes.
Import or export full projects and deliver zipped results with directory structure preserved.

cards.title: Language coverage and model usage options
cards.description: Use the default hosted workflow out of the box, or plug in your team's own model resources and cost strategy.
bullets: Cover 20+ common languages including English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, French, German, and Spanish.
Extend to 100+ languages for both core markets and long-tail localization needs.
Support hosted usage and BYOK to balance unified workflows with flexible configuration.

cards.title: Responsive Web Studio design
cards.description: File management, parameter setup, and translation results stay in one UI so review and comparison are easier.
bullets: The top action bar brings translation triggers, project settings, model settings, and upload entry points together.
The left source pane handles source locale, file path, file type, and original content editing.
The right result pane shows multiple target locales through tabs for quick comparison.

cards.title: Task center
cards.description: For project-level and batch translation workflows, task status, progress, and exceptions are displayed in a dedicated view.
bullets: Track running, queued, paused, completed, and failed tasks in one place.
Show progress, chunk-level processing state, and exception details for long-running work.
Pause or resume tasks to improve operational visibility for teams.

cards.title: Full CLI support across the workflow
cards.description: The CLI is not a side entrance. It is the full sync bridge between local codebases and the online workspace.
bullets: Provide the `jsontranslate` npm package for engineering workflows and local development.
Support init, login, API key setup, upload, translation, status checks, and pull-back of results.
Create one connected workflow between the Web Studio and the CLI.

mapping.title: Import by project, generate by rule, deliver in the original structure
mapping.description: JsonTranslate manages file flows at the project level. Translation results no longer need manual path cleanup and can be generated directly into your existing i18n directory conventions.
bullets: Import project-level source files and organize target locale directories according to the original rules after translation.
Preserve original keys, nesting, and content structure as much as possible during translation so existing i18n organization is not broken.
Download the full project as a ZIP with the original folder hierarchy preserved for direct engineering write-back or team handoff.

zip.title: Batch delivery and ZIP export
zip.description: Export a full directory archive after project translation finishes without flattening the original folder structure. It fits engineering write-back, release delivery, and cross-role collaboration.

languageModel.title: Cover 20+ common languages and support both hosted usage and BYOK
languageModel.description: Use JsonTranslate AI by default for a ready-to-run workflow, or connect your own API keys and multi-provider models. Teams with existing model budgets, cost controls, or stricter compliance needs can plug in their own strategy.

cards.title: Language coverage
cards.description: Built-in support for 20+ common languages including English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, French, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Russian, and Arabic, with expansion to 100+ languages.

cards.title: Hosted workflow
cards.description: Use JsonTranslate AI by default for a more unified experience that suits teams who want to start quickly and manage multilingual work in one place.

cards.title: BYOK
cards.description: Connect your own provider, model, and API key. When you use your own model resources, you can align with existing team cost strategy instead of relying on platform credit consumption.

workspace.title: A responsive workspace keeps files, parameters, and results in one interface
workspace.description: JsonAIStudio uses a translation-first responsive workspace layout. Whether you edit one file or run project-scale batch translation, the whole workflow stays in one interface.

columns.title: Top action bar
columns.description: Centralize translation triggers, project settings, model settings, upload, and import operations.

columns.title: Left source pane
columns.description: Configure source locale, file path, and file type, then edit or inspect original content and change details.

columns.title: Right target pane
columns.description: Switch across target locales through tabs and compare results side by side before exporting or syncing.

taskCenter.title: Status management and scheduling entry point for batch translation

statuses.label: Running
statuses.detail: Show current progress, processed chunks, and remaining locale tasks.

statuses.label: Queued
statuses.detail: Useful for project-scale batch work with one shared execution queue.

statuses.label: Paused
statuses.detail: Interrupt long-running tasks and resume them later when needed.

statuses.label: Failed
statuses.detail: Keep exception details and task context so issues are easier to investigate.

cli.title: Web and CLI stay connected as one localizable delivery loop
cli.description: JsonTranslate does not treat the CLI as a side feature. It is designed as the full synchronization path between your local repository and the online translation workspace.

steps.title: Initialize the local project
steps.description: Configure project name, source locale, target locales, root directory, and path pattern to create a syncable translation context for the codebase.

steps.title: Upload local files and start translation
steps.description: Push source files to the server for unified comparison and processing, then run incremental or full translation by file or by project.

steps.title: Check status and manage tasks
steps.description: Use the CLI or task center to track queued, running, paused, and completed states so execution cadence stays visible.

steps.title: Pull the latest translation results
steps.description: Sync server-side results back into the local project tree and keep the Web and CLI workflow aligned.

pricing.title: Choose the setup that fits your team
pricing.description: Start quickly with the hosted platform mode, or combine custom models, project path mapping, and CLI sync to match your team's resources.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: These answers map directly to the current JsonTranslate workflow, including incremental translation, project path mapping, task scheduling, and CLI sync.

items.question: What file formats does JsonTranslate support?
items.answer: JsonTranslate currently supports JSON, Markdown, and TXT inputs, so teams can translate i18n files, docs, and other plain-text content in one workflow.

items.question: Can JsonTranslate translate only the JSON keys that changed?
items.answer: Yes. For JSON files, JsonTranslate detects added, modified, and deleted content at the key level so incremental translation jobs focus on real changes instead of reprocessing the whole file.

items.question: Can I run translation for a full project instead of a single file?
items.answer: Yes. You can choose file-level incremental or full translation, and you can also run project-level incremental or full-project translation when you need broader delivery.

items.question: How does JsonTranslate handle i18n path mapping?
items.answer: Path mapping is handled at the project level. You define how source and target files should be organized, and JsonTranslate can generate output using patterns such as locale folders, module folders, or locale suffixes.

items.question: Can I import or export translated files as a ZIP?
items.answer: Yes. JsonTranslate preserves directory structure for project workflows, and finished translation results can be exported as a ZIP without flattening the original hierarchy.

items.question: Which languages can I translate into?
items.answer: The hosted workflow covers 20+ common languages out of the box and can extend to 100+ languages for broader localization needs.

items.question: Do I have to use the hosted JsonTranslate AI workflow?
items.answer: No. The hosted workflow is the fastest way to start, but it is not the only option if your team already has its own model access or API setup.

items.question: Can I bring my own API key or model provider?
items.answer: Yes. JsonTranslate supports BYOK, so you can connect your own provider, model, and API key instead of relying only on the default hosted setup.

items.question: How does the task center help with batch translation?
items.answer: The task center keeps queued, running, paused, completed, and failed jobs visible in one place, which makes long-running or project-scale batches easier to track and schedule.

items.question: Is there a CLI for local repositories?
items.answer: Yes. The `jsontranslate` CLI supports init, login, API key setup, upload, translation, status checks, and pull so local repositories can stay connected to the online workspace.

items.question: How do I sync translated files back into my project?
items.answer: After translation finishes, you can pull the latest results back into your local project tree or export the translated project as a ZIP for review and engineering write-back.

items.question: Can I start small before translating the whole project?
items.answer: Yes. Many teams begin with one file or one module, confirm path rules and model settings, and then expand to project-level translation once the workflow looks right.

audience.title: Built for engineering and localization teams that maintain multilingual projects over time
audience.description: If you care about more than one-off translation and need structure, incremental updates, batch delivery, and local synchronization, this workflow is better suited to long-lived multilingual collaboration.
items: Frontend and full-stack teams managing multilingual i18n files
SaaS teams that maintain project directory structures over the long term
Localization teams focused on incremental efficiency and batch delivery
Engineering teams that want online translation capability wired into local development workflows

contact.title: Questions about product capabilities, project workflows, CLI integration, or model support?
contact.description: Talk to JsonTranslate directly. Whether you are evaluating i18n file translation, project-level path mapping, or local repository sync workflows, you can continue through the website or technical support channel.
